articles.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

articles.title: Trump’s Alien.gov Reveal Turns Into Immigration Satire and Leaves UFO Disclosure Community Furious
articles.summary: The launch of Alien.gov, expected by many to be a historic UFO disclosure, instead delivered a political immigration message. The rollout sparked backlash from UFO researchers, disclosure advocates, and ordinary Americans who sought answers. Critics compared the event to former Arizona Governor Fife Symington’s handling of the Phoenix Lights, questioning government credibility. The controversy raises concerns about public trust and transparency.

en.title: Trump’s Alien.gov Reveal Turns Into Immigration Satire and Leaves UFO Disclosure Community Furious
en.summary: The launch of Alien.gov, expected by many to be a historic UFO disclosure, instead delivered a political immigration message. The rollout sparked backlash from UFO researchers, disclosure advocates, and ordinary Americans who sought answers. Critics compared the event to former Arizona Governor Fife Symington’s handling of the Phoenix Lights, questioning government credibility.

de.title: Trumps Alien.gov-Enthüllung wird zur Einwanderungssatire und versetzt die UFO-Enthüllungs-Community in Aufruhr
de.summary: Der Start von Alien.gov, von dem viele eine historische UFO-Enthüllung erwarteten, übermittelte stattdessen eine politische Einwanderungsbotschaft. Die Einführung löste Gegenreaktionen von UFO-Forschern, Offenlegungsbefürwortern und einfachen Amerikanern aus, die nach Antworten suchten. Kritiker verglichen das Ereignis mit dem Umgang des ehemaligen Gouverneurs von Arizona, Fife Symington, mit den Phoenix Lights und stellten die Glaubwürdigkeit der Regierung in Frage.

articles.title: Former Pentagon-Linked Physicist Alleges the United States Has Retrieved Biological Remains From Four Types of Alien Life Forms
articles.summary: Dr. Hal Puthoff, a former CIA‑funded researcher, said the U.S. has recovered four distinct extraterrestrial species from crashed UFOs, citing people involved in the recoveries. Dr. Eric Davis identified the species as Grays, Nordics, Insectoids, and Reptilians, describing each as humanoid with two arms and legs, and noting that Nordics and Reptilians are about six feet tall. Nordics are described as highly human‑like and resembling northern Europeans, while Reptilians are scaley, lizard‑like with human limbs and long tails; Grays are small, massive‑eyed, hairless, and Insectoids resemble mantises. Former Air Force Intelligence officer David Grusch testified in 2023 that the U.S. possessed non‑human biologics recovered from dozens of crashed UFOs. These claims are based on testimony from members of a crash‑retrieval program featured in Dan Farrah’s film “Age of Disclosure,” and Farrah reported that interviewees have declined to speak publicly.

articles.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna Frames UFO Disclosure Through The Book of Enoch As A Window Into A Reality Beyond Human Perception
articles.summary: Florida Representative Anna Paulina Luna publicly linked the ongoing UAP discussion to themes in the Book of Enoch, suggesting a possible connection between unidentified aerial phenomena and ancient accounts of heavenly beings. Luna’s remarks contrast with the evidence‑driven tone that has characterized recent disclosure efforts, but she frames the link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland analogy, which explain how beings from other dimensions might appear incomprehensible. The modern UAP conversation, recognized by NASA and the Department of Defense, includes documented observations of objects that accelerate suddenly, travel across media, and behave in ways that do not match known propulsion or aerodynamics. The Book of Enoch describes Watchers who descend from the heavens, interact with humanity, and impart advanced knowledge, and Luna notes structural parallels between these narratives and the interdimensional hypothesis, though she does not claim the texts provide scientific evidence. At present there is no verified evidence that UAPs are linked to the entities described in the Book of Enoch; the connection remains speculative and will depend on future evidence. The discussion around UFOs has broadened to include questions of reality, perception, and long‑standing encounters with the unknown, challenging policymakers to distinguish belief, theory, and evidence.

en.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna Frames UFO Disclosure Through The Book of Enoch As A Window Into A Reality Beyond Human Perception
en.summary: Florida Representative Anna Paulina Luna publicly linked the ongoing UAP discussion to themes in the Book of Enoch, suggesting a possible connection between unidentified aerial phenomena and ancient accounts of heavenly beings. Luna’s remarks contrast with the evidence‑driven tone that has characterized recent disclosure efforts, but she frames the link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland analogy, which explain how beings from other dimensions might appear incomprehensible.

de.title: Die Abgeordnete Anna Paulina Luna stellt die UFO-Enthüllung anhand des Buches Henoch als Fenster in eine Realität dar, die jenseits der menschlichen Wahrnehmung liegt
de.summary: Die Vertreterin von Florida, Anna Paulina Luna, hat die laufende UAP-Diskussion öffentlich mit Themen im Buch Henoch in Verbindung gebracht und einen möglichen Zusammenhang zwischen unbekannten Luftphänomenen und alten Berichten über himmlische Wesen angedeutet. Lunas Bemerkungen stehen im Gegensatz zu dem evidenzbasierten Ton, der die jüngsten Enthüllungsbemühungen geprägt hat, aber sie formuliert den Zusammenhang durch die interdimensionale Hypothese und Carl Sagans Flatland-Analogie, die erklären, wie Wesen aus anderen Dimensionen unverständlich erscheinen könnten.

articles.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
articles.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Lawmakers host whistleblowers, UFO investigators to press feds to come clean about space aliens
